questions.q: Girish Karnad was best known as a playwright, actor and director in which Indian language as his primary literary medium?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad wrote his celebrated plays primarily in Kannada, though they were widely translated and staged in many languages.

questions.q: In which year was the Kannada film Samskara, co-directed by Girish Karnad, released?
questions.explanation: Samskara, based on U. R. Ananthamurthy's novel and co-directed by Karnad, was released in 1970.

questions.q: The 1970 film Samskara, associated with Girish Karnad, is credited with helping launch which movement?
questions.explanation: Samskara is widely regarded as a film that helped launch the Kannada New Wave of parallel cinema.

questions.q: Which prestigious literary award did Girish Karnad receive in 1998?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad was awarded the Jnanpith Award in 1998, India's highest literary honour, for his contribution to drama.

questions.q: In which year was the playwright and filmmaker Girish Karnad born?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad was born on 19 May 1938.

questions.q: In which year did Girish Karnad pass away?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad died on 10 June 2019 in Bengaluru.

questions.q: Besides being a playwright, in which other major creative role was Girish Karnad widely active in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad acted widely across Kannada and Hindi films in addition to his work as a playwright and director.

questions.q: Which of these is one of Girish Karnad's most famous plays, based on a tale from the Mahabharata?
questions.explanation: Yayati, drawn from a story in the Mahabharata, was Girish Karnad's first play.

questions.q: Which highly acclaimed Girish Karnad play centres on the 14th-century Delhi Sultanate ruler Muhammad bin Tughlaq?
questions.explanation: Karnad's play Tughlaq, about Sultan Muhammad bin Tughlaq, is one of his most celebrated and frequently staged works.

questions.q: The 1970 film Samskara, co-directed by Girish Karnad, won a National Film Award in which category?
options: Best Feature Film (Swarna Kamal / President's Gold Medal)
questions.explanation: Samskara won the President's Gold Medal (the National Award for the best feature film of the year).

questions.q: Samskara (1970), which featured Girish Karnad, was based on a novel by which Kannada author?
questions.explanation: Samskara was adapted from the novel of the same name by U. R. Ananthamurthy, with Karnad writing the screenplay and playing the lead.

questions.q: In the 1970 film Samskara, what role did Girish Karnad play in addition to co-directing and writing it?
questions.explanation: Karnad played the lead role of Praneshacharya and wrote the screenplay for Samskara.

questions.q: Which prestigious international scholarship did Girish Karnad win, taking him to Oxford University?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad studied at the University of Oxford as a Rhodes Scholar.

questions.q: Which 1976 Shyam Benegal film featuring Girish Karnad dramatized India's dairy cooperative (milk) movement?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad starred in Shyam Benegal's Manthan (1976), a landmark film about the dairy cooperative movement.

questions.q: Girish Karnad acted alongside Smita Patil in which 1977 Shyam Benegal film about a Marathi stage and screen actress?
questions.explanation: Karnad appeared in Shyam Benegal's Bhumika (1977), in which Smita Patil played the lead inspired by actress Hansa Wadkar.

questions.q: Which national civilian honour was conferred on Girish Karnad by the Government of India?
options: The Padma Shri and the Padma Bhushan
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad received both the Padma Shri (1974) and the Padma Bhushan (1992) for his contributions to the arts.

questions.q: Girish Karnad's play Hayavadana draws on the theme of a transposed head, partly inspired by stories from which classical source?
options: The Vetala tales (Kathasaritsagara / Thomas Mann's retelling)
questions.explanation: Hayavadana is based on a tale from the Kathasaritsagara, also retold by Thomas Mann in The Transposed Heads.

questions.q: Girish Karnad hosted a science-based television programme on Doordarshan in the 1980s. What was its theme?
questions.explanation: Karnad hosted the popular science programme Turning Point on Doordarshan in the late 1980s and early 1990s.

questions.q: In the 2000s Hindi film series Ek Tha Tiger and Tiger Zinda Hai, Girish Karnad played which kind of role?
questions.explanation: Karnad played Shenoy, the RAW chief, in the Salman Khan spy films Ek Tha Tiger (2012) and Tiger Zinda Hai (2017).

questions.q: Girish Karnad directed and starred in the 1971 Kannada film Vamsha Vriksha, based on a novel by which author?
questions.explanation: Vamsha Vriksha (1971), co-directed by Karnad and B. V. Karanth, was based on S. L. Bhyrappa's novel and won a National Award.

questions.q: Girish Karnad served as the director of which national film institution in the 1970s?
options: Film and Television Institute of India (FTII), Pune
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad served as the director of the Film and Television Institute of India (FTII) in Pune during the 1970s.

questions.q: In which 1975 Shyam Benegal film about feudal oppression did Girish Karnad act as a schoolteacher whose wife is abducted?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad played the schoolteacher in Shyam Benegal's Nishant (1975), a key film of the Indian parallel cinema movement.

questions.q: Girish Karnad made his acting and screenwriting debut in films with the 1970 Kannada movie Samskara, but in which Indian state was he born?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad was born in Matheran, in present-day Maharashtra, though he is most associated with Kannada culture.

questions.q: Girish Karnad's play Taledanda (Death by Beheading) is set against the 12th-century social reform movement led by which figure?
questions.explanation: Taledanda is set during the 12th-century Lingayat/Sharana movement led by Basavanna in Karnataka.

questions.q: Which 1978 Kannada film directed by Girish Karnad was a samurai-style period drama set in a medieval era?
questions.explanation: Ondanondu Kaladalli (1978), directed by Karnad, was a medieval action drama inspired by Japanese samurai cinema.

questions.q: In Nagesh Kukunoor's 2005 Hindi film Iqbal, about a deaf-mute aspiring cricketer, Girish Karnad played which character?
questions.explanation: In Iqbal (2005), Girish Karnad played Guruji, the influential cricket-academy coach and former India captain who first admits and then expels the deaf-mute hero Iqbal.

questions.q: In the Kannada film Kaadu (1973), based on a novel by Srikrishna Alanahalli, Girish Karnad served in which capacity?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad directed Kaadu (1973), a film about village feuds based on Srikrishna Alanahalli's novel.

questions.q: Girish Karnad played Jawaharlal Nehru in which Shyam Benegal television series based on Nehru's 'The Discovery of India'?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad portrayed Jawaharlal Nehru in Shyam Benegal's television series Bharat Ek Khoj, adapted from Nehru's The Discovery of India.

questions.q: Girish Karnad's English-language memoir of his early years was published under which title?
questions.explanation: Karnad's autobiography, translated into English as 'This Life at Play', recounts his early life and entry into theatre and film.

questions.q: Which lavish 1984 Hindi period film, based on the Sanskrit play Mrichchhakatika, was directed by Girish Karnad?
questions.explanation: Girish Karnad directed Utsav (1984), a Hindi period drama based on the ancient Sanskrit play Mrichchhakatika (The Little Clay Cart).
